Player.title: Player
Player.description: Represents a player's account in Axiom Frontier, managing their game progress and associated agents.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Player entity.

username.description: The player's chosen display name within the game.

email.description: The player's email address (for contact purposes, not authentication).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the player's account was created.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp indicating the player's last login.

agentIds.description: References to Agents controlled by this player. (Relationship: Player 1:N Agent)

Agent.title: Agent
Agent.description: An in-game character that performs actions like gathering, combat, trading, and exploring, controlled by a Player or as an NPC.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Agent entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the Player who owns this agent. (Relationship: Player 1:N Agent)

positionX.description: The agent's current X-coordinate in the game world.

positionY.description: The agent's current Y-coordinate in the game world.

positionZ.description: The agent's current Z-coordinate in the game world.

energy.description: The agent's current energy points, used for actions.

inventoryItemIds.description: References to Item entities representing items in the agent's inventory. (Relationship: Agent 1:N Item - conceptual via references to item definitions)

currentQuestId.description: Reference to the Quest entity the agent is currently undertaking. (Relationship: Quest 1:N Agent)

WorldState.title: WorldState
WorldState.description: A singleton entity representing the global state of the game world, updated by the world heartbeat.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorldState entity. Expected to be 'current' as a singleton.

tick.description: The current game tick or cycle number, incremented by the world heartbeat.

civilizationIndex.description: A calculated index reflecting the overall civilization level and progress in the game world.

totalCurrency.description: The total amount of in-game currency currently in circulation within the economy.

lastHeartbeatAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the world heartbeat last updated this state.

Quest.title: Quest
Quest.description: A dynamically generated task or mission available to agents in the game world.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quest entity.

description.description: A full description of the quest, potentially generated by AI.

rewardItemIds.description: References to Item entities that are rewarded upon quest completion.

status.description: The current status of the quest, e.g., 'Available', 'InProgress', 'Completed'.

generatedByAI.description: Indicates if the quest content was dynamically generated by an AI.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the quest was created.

assignedToNpcIds.description: References to NPC entities that offer or are associated with this quest. (Relationship: NPC N:N Quest)

NPC.title: NPC
NPC.description: A Non-Player Character with a unique personality and role in the game world, potentially AI-generated.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NPC entity.

personalityDescription.description: An AI-generated description of the NPC's personality traits and quirks.

dialogueTreeId.description: Reference to a DialogueTree or script entity defining the NPC's conversational responses.

positionX.description: The NPC's current X-coordinate in the game world.

positionY.description: The NPC's current Y-coordinate in the game world.

positionZ.description: The NPC's current Z-coordinate in the game world.

assignedQuestIds.description: References to Quest entities that this NPC offers or is involved with. (Relationship: NPC N:N Quest)

Item.title: Item
Item.description: A tangible or intangible asset in the game, which can be acquired, used, traded, or consumed.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Item entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the item and its properties.

type.description: The category of the item, e.g., 'Weapon', 'Consumable', 'Currency', 'QuestItem'.

value.description: The numeric value of the item for trading or economic calculations.

assetId.description: Reference to the GameAsset entity for its visual or audio representation in the game. (Relationship: GameAsset 1:N Item)

stackable.description: Indicates if multiple instances of this item can be stacked together in an inventory slot.

maxStackSize.description: The maximum number of this item that can be stacked together, if stackable.

GameAsset.title: GameAsset
GameAsset.description: Metadata for a 3D model, texture, or sound file stored in Firebase Storage, used by the Godot client.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GameAsset entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the game asset.

storagePath.description: The path or URI to the asset file in Firebase Storage.

version.description: The version identifier for the asset, allowing for updates.

metadata.description: An array of metadata tags or properties associated with the asset.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records financial transactions within the Axiom Storefront, such as purchases of game items or currency.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the Player who initiated the transaction. (Relationship: Player 1:N Transaction)

itemId.description: Reference to the Item entity purchased, if the transaction involves a specific item. (Relationship: Item 1:N Transaction)

currencyAmount.description: The amount of in-game currency involved in the transaction, if applicable.

realMoneyAmount.description: The amount of real-world money involved in the transaction.

currencyType.description: The type of real-world currency used (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

paymentMethod.description: The payment method used for the transaction, e.g., 'PayPal'.

status.description: The current status of the transaction, e.g., 'Pending', 'Completed', 'Failed'.

definition.description: Each document represents a player's profile in Axiom Frontier. The `playerId` in the path corresponds to `request.auth.uid`, ensuring direct ownership and simplifying security rules. Only the authenticated player can read/write their own document.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Player, which matches Firebase Authentication's UID.

definition.description: Agents owned by a specific player. The `playerId` in the path segment directly links to the owning player, ensuring strong path-based ownership. Includes denormalized 'playerId' field for authorization independence, allowing security rules to verify `request.auth.uid == playerId` without `get()` calls. Only the owning player can manage their agents.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the owning Player.

params.name: agentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Agent.

definition.description: Records of financial transactions made by a player in the Axiom Storefront. The `playerId` in the path segment ensures strong path-based ownership. Includes denormalized 'playerId' field for authorization independence. Only the owning player can read/write their own transactions, with admin users having read access for auditing.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Player who initiated the transaction.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Transaction.

definition.description: A singleton document holding the global game state, updated by a scheduled Cloud Function (world heartbeat). Provides public read access for game clients, with write access restricted to the Cloud Function service account or specific admin rules.
Global definitions for dynamically generated quests. All quests are publicly readable by clients. Write and update access is restricted to administrative users or AI-powered Cloud Functions.

params.name: questId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Quest.

definition.description: Global definitions and current states for Non-Player Characters, potentially AI-generated. Publicly readable by clients, with write/update access restricted to administrative users or AI-powered Cloud Functions.

params.name: npcId
params.description: The unique identifier of the NPC.

definition.description: Global definitions for all in-game items. Publicly readable by clients. Write access is restricted to administrative users or game logic Cloud Functions.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Item.

definition.description: Metadata for game assets (e.g., 3D models, textures, sounds) stored in Firebase Storage. Publicly readable for clients to fetch asset references. Write access is restricted to administrative users.

params.name: assetId
params.description: The unique identifier of the GameAsset.

definition.description: Database-backed access control for administrative users. The existence of a document at this path for a given `uid` signifies administrative privileges. Used by security rules for `exists()` checks. This collection is typically not directly readable/writable by regular users, only by a super-admin or dedicated admin function.

params.name: uid
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who has an administrative role.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable access patterns by adhering strictly to the core design principles. The primary strategy employed is **Authorization Independence via Denormalization** and **Structural Segregation**. 

1.  **Player-Owned Data (Authorization Independence):** Player-specific entities like `Agent` and `Transaction` are structured as subcollections under individual player documents (`/players/{playerId}/agents/{agentId}` and `/players/{playerId}/transactions/{transactionId}`). This hierarchical path directly embeds the `playerId` in the document path. Consequently, Firestore security rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `{playerId}` wildcard in the path, eliminating the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls to parent documents for ownership verification. This ensures atomic operations, simplifies rule logic, and enhances debuggability. The `playerId` field is also explicitly denormalized within the `Agent` and `Transaction` documents themselves, further solidifying the ownership invariant and facilitating direct queries if needed outside the parent context (though primary authorization will rely on the path).

2.  **Global Data Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture & QAPs):** Entities with global or public read access, such as `WorldState`, `Quest`, `NPC`, `Item`, and `GameAsset`, are placed in top-level collections (e.g., `/quests/{questId}`). Each of these collections exhibits a homogeneous security posture: public read for all clients (crucial for QAPs - enabling secure `list` operations without client-side filtering) and restricted write access for administrative users or specific Cloud Functions (e.g., AI content generation, world heartbeat updates). This segregation prevents mixing data with different access requirements within the same collection, simplifying security rules immensely by moving authorization complexity from rule logic to the structural layout of the data.

3.  **Singleton `WorldState`:** The `WorldState` is designed as a singleton document at `/world_state/current`. This ensures a single source of truth for global game parameters and allows for direct, efficient access while maintaining tight write control by the world heartbeat Cloud Function.

4.  **DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control):** A dedicated collection `/roles_admin/{uid}` is proposed for administrative roles. The mere existence of a document at this path for a user's UID signifies their administrative status. Security rules can efficiently check `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` to grant elevated privileges, adhering to the principle of using database-stored roles over custom claims for simpler management and debuggability.

This design effectively supports all core principles, making the Firestore structure robust, secure, and easy to manage from both development and operational perspectives.
